title: pascalia subvaginata
intro: Pascalia subvaginata, also known as Mexican prickly poppy or Mexican poppy, is a plant species that belongs to the family Malvaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and treating conditions like fever and skin irritations. Pascalia subvaginata can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
